Creating images with R

A coding meme

Menghan Shi

2026-03-17

body { background-color: #f5f5f5; }
h2 { color: darkblue; }
p { font-size: 16px; }

## Introduction

In this project, I created images and memes using R. I used the `magick` package to read, edit, and annotate images. This project helped me understand how to manipulate images programmatically.

## Project requirements

![](project_folder.png)

[My GitHub repo](https://github.com/mshi417-lgtm/stats220)

## Inspo meme
![](my_meme.png)

This meme was found online and used as inspiration for my design. It uses a common meme format with text at the top and bottom to express a relatable situation.
---


## My meme

Below is the R code I used to create a static meme:

library(magick)
## Linking to ImageMagick 6.9.13.29
## Enabled features: cairo, fontconfig, freetype, heic, lcms, pango, raw, rsvg, webp
## Disabled features: fftw, ghostscript, x11
img <- image_read("my_meme.png")

meme <- img %>%
  image_annotate("WHEN YOUR CODE HAS NO ERRORS",
                 size = 45,
                 color = "white",
                 strokecolor = "black",
                 gravity = "north",
                 location = "+0+20") %>%
  image_annotate("AND YOU DON'T KNOW WHY",
                 size = 45,
                 color = "white",
                 strokecolor = "black",
                 gravity = "south",
                 location = "+0+20")

meme

In my version of the meme, I changed the text to reflect the experience of coding, which many students can relate to.

My animated meme

Below is the animated meme I created:

Creativity

This project demonstrates creativity through the design of both static and animated memes. I used different text placement and content to create humour and make the meme more engaging.

Learning reflection

In this project, I learned how to create HTML documents by combining R, Markdown, and CSS. One important idea I learned is how these technologies work together to produce a complete output. R is used to generate images, Markdown helps organise the content, and CSS controls the design.

I also learned how to create static and animated memes using the magick package, which helped me understand how programming can be used creatively.

In the future, I am interested in learning more about web design, especially CSS and interactive features. I also want to explore more R packages for visualisation and web development.
